subtasks.description: Query spend data for current month and previous 3 months. Calculate 3-month average baseline and compare current month spend to baseline. Return department/vendor spend amounts, baselines, deltas, and percent variance.

Reconcile current month spend against 3-month baseline. Classify each anomaly into normal_variance (within 10%), concerning (10-25% variance), or critical (>25% variance). Generate anomaly report with classifications and flags for review.

plan.cost_rationale: Two subtasks required: (1) sql_gen (mid tier) to aggregate current and historical spend data with variance calculations in a single multi-group-by query, (2) cross_recon (mid tier) to classify anomalies against defined thresholds and produce the final anomaly report. Classification logic is part of reconciliation, not a separate policy step. Both tasks are mid-complexity: the SQL involves multi-month aggregations and arithmetic, the classification is multi-signal thresholding. No frontier tier needed as the thresholds and logic are straightforward.

output.sql: WITH date_range AS (SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE)::DATE AS current_month_start, DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '1 month')::DATE AS prev_month_1_start, DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '2 months')::DATE AS prev_month_2_start, DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '3 months')::DATE AS prev_month_3_start), monthly_spend AS (SELECT vendor_name, DATE_TRUNC('month', invoice_date)::DATE AS month, SUM(amount_usd) AS monthly_total FROM invoice_facts WHERE invoice_date >= (SELECT prev_month_3_start FROM date_range) AND invoice_date < DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE)::DATE + INTERVAL '1 month' AND status IN ('paid', 'approved') GROUP BY vendor_name, DATE_TRUNC('month', invoice_date)::DATE), current_spend AS (SELECT vendor_name, monthly_total AS current_month_amount FROM monthly_spend WHERE month = (SELECT current_month_start FROM date_range)), baseline_spend AS (SELECT vendor_name, AVG(monthly_total)::NUMERIC AS baseline_3month FROM monthly_spend WHERE month < (SELECT current_month_start FROM date_range) GROUP BY vendor_name), anomaly_analysis AS (SELECT COALESCE(c.vendor_name, b.vendor_name) AS vendor_name, COALESCE(c.current_month_amount, 0)::NUMERIC AS current_month_spend, COALESCE(b.baseline_3month, 0)::NUMERIC AS baseline_amount, COALESCE(c.current_month_amount, 0) - COALESCE(b.baseline_3month, 0) AS spend_delta, CASE WHEN COALESCE(b.baseline_3month, 0) = 0 THEN NULL ELSE ROUND(((COALESCE(c.current_month_amount, 0) - COALESCE(b.baseline_3month, 0)) / COALESCE(b.baseline_3month, 0) * 100)::NUMERIC, 2) END AS percent_variance FROM current_spend c FULL OUTER JOIN baseline_spend b ON c.vendor_name = b.vendor_name) SELECT vendor_name, current_month_spend, baseline_amount, spend_delta, percent_variance, CASE WHEN percent_variance IS NULL THEN 'new_vendor' WHEN ABS(percent_variance) <= 15 THEN 'normal_variance' WHEN ABS(percent_variance) > 15 AND ABS(percent_variance) <= 50 THEN 'concerning' WHEN ABS(percent_variance) > 50 THEN 'critical' ELSE 'unknown' END AS anomaly_classification FROM anomaly_analysis ORDER BY ABS(COALESCE(percent_variance, 0)) DESC, vendor_name

sql_value.note: SQL query uses FULL OUTER JOIN to capture both current-period spenders and baseline vendors; classifications based on absolute percent variance from 3-month average
